label: Growing trend: measured negative reads as zero
verdict: Read this as zero, not negative. The model puts the campaign effect at −130K streams (95% credible interval −150K to −100K), below zero, but an ad campaign cannot produce fewer streams than no campaign at all, so a measured negative effect is not evidence the ad hurt. It signals that something outside the data moved streams down during the campaign window and the model could not control for it, so confidence is low and the honest campaign attribution is read as ZERO. The Campaign impact table below still shows the measured numbers for full transparency.
